videos.title: 9 POWERFUL Beetroot & Ginger Juice Health Benefits You Need to Know | How To Make it

transcript.fullText: the beautiful bright color of beetroot can make anyone fall in love with them yet they're ignored perhaps because of their distinctive taste if you're not a fan you might want to think of an easier way to still enjoy the many health benefits of beetroot without having to actually eat it meat roots are best enjoyed raw and uncooked and juicing them up is a quick and simple method of consuming them add ginger to the mix which is known for being one of the most powerful natural medicines in the world and you're on to a winner here's everything you need to know about the health benefits of beetroot and ginger juice one it manages your blood pressure one of the benefits of beetroot juice and ginger is that it can help in temporarily lowering high blood pressure one reason for this is the presence of naturally occurring nitrates in beets which increase nitric oxide in the blood vessels allowing more oxygen to flow to your brain heart and muscles the anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger can also help manage blood pressure one study review found that ginger can lower blood pressure and decrease blood fat levels both of which help protect against heart disease two it gives you glowing skin if you're healthy from within it reflects on the outside beetroot and ginger juice act as a great blood purifier which is key in keeping your skin glowing and healthy meat roots are also rich in vitamin c which helps in clearing blemishes and evens out your skin tone while giving in a natural glow three it's a great way to detox naturally beet roots are a unique source of phytonutrients these compounds are known for their powerful antioxidant anti-inflammatory and detoxification properties for this reason beetroot juice in particular is a great liver cleanser and helps to flush out toxins ginger has also proved an effective detoxing food with some studies linking it to benefits and weight loss and there's definitely some promising research linking ginger to weight management 4. boost energy and stamina beetroot and ginger juice helps to open the blood vessels and increase the flow of oxygen throughout your body making you feel more energetic and active that's why so many people prefer to drink this juice in the morning so they can enjoy an energized start to the day it has often been promoted as a great workout drink and good for athletes thanks to its ability to increase oxygen to the muscles preparing them for the stress ahead 5. good for digestion beetroot and ginger juice is full of fiber which helps to regulate digestive processes and relieve constipation beetroot is also beneficial for good digestive health it's thought that beetroot increases stomach acid levels a glass of beetroot and ginger juice is also a great home remedy for an upset stomach research has linked multiple digestive benefits to ginger specifically feelings of nausea stomach upset and vomiting it may also help move food from the stomach to the small intestine for digestion and absorption 6. lowers blood glucose diabetes is a huge problem around the world and while drinking beetroot and ginger juice is far from a diabetes cure it may help someone in preventing and managing it research has linked some active compounds in ginger with improvements in insulin and metabolism and though high in sugar beetroot actually helps in regulating your blood sugar levels studies have shown that it doesn't lead to high glucose concentration in the blood the natural sugars in beet root are released very slowly in the body preventing sudden spikes 7. it's an anti-inflammatory like other produce ginger and beets contain antioxidant-like compounds that may reduce cell damage the root of ginger can also prevent inflammation by reducing cell signaling activity another reason to drink the two together eight can curb morning sickness pregnant women take note ginger and beetroot juice may help reduce symptoms of morning sickness research has found that it's safe to take ginger during pregnancy and it has shown improvement in morning sickness symptoms when compared to a placebo nine may lower your risk of cancer the cell protecting properties of ginger can lower the long-term risk of certain cancers that's because the spice and other flavorings can reduce cellular activity that causes dna changes cell death and proliferation of cancer cells so how can you make beetroot and ginger juice well it's quick and easy though you will need a juicer or a blender for the job start by washing and scrubbing your beets and ginger peel and cut the beets and ginger into small pieces that you can feed into the juicer many people also choose to squeeze a little lemon juice but that's completely up to you stir well and drink up if you only have a blender don't panic you can still make the juice with it just remove the peels from your ingredients blend until smooth and then strain through a nut milk bag or fine mesh sieve squeezing to get out as much juice as possible if you don't currently start your day with beetroot and ginger juice what are you waiting for this simple concoction is incredibly helpful so it's definitely worth adding to your diet sooner rather than later i hope you've enjoyed this video if you have please subscribe to this youtube channel and don't forget to give this video the thumbs up and i'm sure you're going to enjoy this next video on six health benefits of baking soda and apple cider vinegar tonic drink thanks for watching and bye for now you

videos.title: What Happens To Your Body When You Eat Ginger Everyday

transcript.fullText: all right [Music] what happens to your body when you eat ginger every day Ginger is a spice that has been used for over 4 000 years it's a very powerful herb that helps to treat or prevent many types of diseases this popular spice was originally found in both Indian Cuisine and traditional Chinese medicine the distinctive taste of ginger is produced by its active ingredient gingerol which shows antibacterial and antioxidant properties in addition to affecting your taste buds Ginger affects numerous other bodily functions here are a few notable health benefits of eating ginger before we proceed please note that everything mentioned in this video is unbiased fact checked and reviewed by qualified health professionals with that said let's jump in and talk about the nine benefits of ginger number one eases nausea ginger has traditionally been used in Chinese medicine as a medication to prevent vomiting and as a digestive Aid it's believed that Ginger helps alleviate nausea by stimulating the production of saliva and gastric juices in the stomach which helps digest food more efficiently the herb may also act as an anti-spasmodic agent which is a substance that controls muscle spasms this may help relax smooth muscles within the digestive tract Ginger contains compounds called gingerols show Galls zingerons and other aromatic oils that are thought to be responsible for their anti-nausea properties this spice has also been found to have anti-inflammatory effects in the gastrointestinal tract which may be another reason why it can help with nausea to get rid of nausea try drinking ginger tea ginger tea can be prepared by adding one teaspoon of grated fresh ginger root to one cup of boiling water let it cool for 10 minutes before drinking you can drink this remedy two or three times a day when experiencing mild nausea or vomiting associated with pregnancy or motion sickness If you experience severe nausea or vomiting see your doctor immediately they can recommend the best treatment option for your situation number two boosts the immune system adding Ginger to your diet can help boost the immune system by increasing circulation throughout the body which helps your body fight off infections Ginger contains various compounds such as gingerol and shogol which have been shown to stimulate the body's production of natural killer cells these cells are very important elements of your immune system gingerol has been found to increase the activity of white blood cells which are necessary for fighting infection this compound also increases the production of macrophages which eliminate foreign and harmful substances from the body this helps ward off bacteria viruses and other Invaders that could cause illness or disease researchers also showed that shogol can prevent certain types of cells from dying when exposed to viruses such as influenza A and rhinoviruses which cause colds number three helps ease migraines ginger has been used as a home remedy for migraines for centuries it's believed to work by inhibiting inflammatory enzymes in the body that are thought to play a role in migraines in addition it may help relax muscles and reduce swelling in blood vessels which can ease the pain to get the most benefit from Ginger you should try eating it raw or dried or adding it to your favorite recipes you can also buy Ginger supplements at most pharmacies and health food stores Ginger also contains shogao which has been shown to decrease blood flow in the brain this may help ease migraine pain by reducing blood flow to sensitive areas of your brain liking our video so far before we move on to the next benefit don't forget to hit the like button and subscribe to our channel so you never miss out on our new informative videos number four it may lower the risk of cancer cancer is a terrifying disease it kills more than half a million people each year in the United States alone and it often strikes people in their prime years although there are many forms of cancer they have all one thing in common they're caused by uncontrolled cell growth for example skin cancer is caused by damaged cells that don't die off when they should in some cases the damaged cells grow into tumors that can spread to other parts of the body researchers have found that Ginger contains antioxidants and phytochemicals that may help fight cancer cells in the body in one study researchers found that extracts from Ginger affected prostate cancer cells in vitro they also found that they could lower the risk of prostate cancer by up to 40 percent if people ate three grams per day of powdered ginger root in a study published in the Journal of cancer prevention research scientists examined the effects of ginger on mice with breast cancer they found that Ginger extract reduced the growth of tumors by about 50 percent and significantly reduced their size the researchers then looked at human breast cancer cells in a Petri dish and found that they were less likely to spread their tentacles when exposed to Ginger extract this suggests that Ginger could be an effective treatment for metastatic breast cancer which is often fatal because it spreads throughout the body number five manages high blood sugar levels ginger has been used for centuries to help manage high blood sugar in fact ginger has been found to help lower glucose levels in people with type 2 diabetes when taken in capsule form this is because its active compounds such as gingerols and shogols help stimulate the pancreas to produce more insulin it also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the body which is one of the main causes of type 2 diabetes in one study published in 2009 researchers found that Ginger improved glucose tolerance by increasing insulin secretion from the pancreas and improving insulin sensitivity the study involved 16 healthy volunteers who were given either 500 milligrams of ginger powder or a placebo before meals three times daily for 40 days the results showed that those who took Ginger had significantly lower post-prandial blood glucose levels than those who did not take it number six helps ease anxiety one of the most common reasons people seek medical help is anxiety anxiety is a mental health condition that causes feelings of fear nervousness or worry while some anxiety is normal and even helpful at times it can become overwhelming and interfere with your daily life if it continues for too long or gets out of control the good news is that there are many ways to manage anxiety including exercise and diet changes for example experts suggest that eating foods high in fiber and omega-3 fatty acids may help reduce anxiety symptoms Ginger is another natural remedy for managing anxiety it has been used as an herbal remedy for centuries in Asia and Africa to help with calmness and reduce feelings of nervousness number seven it may help reduce indigestion indigestion is a common problem that many people experience after eating symptoms include bloating gas frequent burping and heartburn these symptoms often come on quickly after eating and last for several hours or days after the meal Ginger is one of several herbs used as a natural remedy for indigestion other herbs that may help include fennel seeds peppermint leaves basil leaves and chamomile flowers Ginger also contains gingerols which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the pain caused by indigestion gingerols can even help reduce the production of gastric acid in the stomach which helps treat mild cases of stomach ulcers when it comes to reducing indigestion Ginger can be taken in the form of tea or added to food or drinks you can also find supplements that contain concentrated amounts of Ginger for maximum effectiveness number eight it may help with asthma asthma is a chronic lung disease that makes it difficult to breathe the Airways which are tubes that carry air into your lungs become swollen and narrow causing shortness of breath wheezing and coughing although there's no cure for asthma many people can manage their symptoms well enough to control their asthma without using inhalers or other medications on a daily basis the ginger all found in ginger helps to relax muscles in the Airways of the lungs this helps reduce swelling and makes it easier for you to breathe it also reduces mucus production in your lungs which makes it easier to cough up sputum if you have trouble breathing try juicing two to three inches of fresh ginger root and drink it as soon as possible you can also make tea with fresh ginger root by putting one teaspoon of grated ginger root into one cup of water and boiling it for 5 minutes number nine it may help to decrease the risk of Alzheimer's disease Ginger helps to decrease the risk of Alzheimer's Disease by inhibiting the formation of plaques in the brain plaques are deposits of amyloid beta peptide on the brain's nerve cells which eventually cause Alzheimer's disease the results from a study showed that Ginger extract inhibited a beta production by up to 80 percent in lab tests gingerol also helps to reduce oxidative stress in the brain by inhibiting enzymes such as cycloxygenase II and five lipogenase these enzymes are responsible for producing inflammatory compounds in the brain which can lead to Alzheimer's disease the decrease in antioxidant enzymes such as catalase and glutathione peroxidase also contributes to oxidative stress because they help remove free radicals from the body are there any health risks with Ginger Ginger is considered to be a safe food and has no known side effects when consumed in moderation however it may interact with some medications and other herbal supplements Ginger may also lower blood sugar levels and affect the way your body uses other herbs vitamins or medication some people experience mild gastrointestinal symptoms like bloating nausea and diarrhea after consuming Ginger people who have gallstones should avoid using Ginger because it can worsen symptoms it's important to talk with your doctor before taking Ginger if you have diabetes or a bleeding disorder such as hemophilia or if you're taking any medications for these conditions Ginger may also interact with certain heart medications so check with your doctor before adding it to your diet if you're on any of these medications as you can see there are many things that Ginger is good for and if you add this seasonal treat to your daily diet you may find that it relieves a number of symptoms and discomfort by covering all the bases Ginger is a versatile remedy that belongs in every health conscious person's kitchen more than that it has anti-inflammatory properties so it's an excellent addition to dieters toolkits enjoyed this video hit the like button to subscribe to our channel for more informative videos like this one thanks for watching your Wellness is our mission foreign [Music]

videos.title: 🫚 How to Juice Ginger & Turmeric | Kuvings AUTO10 Plus Juicer

transcript.fullText: Tips for Juicing Ginger & Turmeric Ginger and turmeric are commonly juiced in small amounts as part of a recipe and typically pose no issues. But, if you want to juice ginger and turmeric in larger amounts or on their own, you may run into issues with clogging. 1. Ginger Ginger and turmeric are fibrous ingredients with ginger being more fibrous than turmeric. A buildup of their long stringy fibers can cause clogging. The key is to chop ginger in the opposite direction of its fibers to break them up. We recommend cutting ginger into small chunks, about 1-2 inches. Juicing chopped ginger is quicker, more efficient, and puts less strain on your juicer’s motor. While juicing ginger, you may notice that juice produces quickly and little pulp exits the spout. This is normal. Ginger is high in water content and light in weight, so there's not much pressure pushing ginger pulp through the juicer. 2. Turmeric Turmeric is smaller and less fibrous than ginger, so it doesn’t need to be chopped. But, it needs to be juiced with lemon so your juicer doesn’t become heavily stained from the curcumin in turmeric. You should have no issues juicing whole turmeric. But in case you do, you can chop it using the same method as ginger. As you saw, we juiced both ginger and turmeric with their peels intact. You don’t need to peel them because their peels are thin and don’t cause clogging. If you'd rather peel your ginger, using a spoon is a great option as it can reach into the crevices and remove the skin without wasting the inner flesh. Turmeric is a bit easier to peel, so it can be peeled normally with a peeler. Just be mindful of turmeric staining your hands. If you’re juicing a lot of ginger and turmeric with other ingredients, layer them at the bottom of the hopper and place hard ingredients on top. The hard ingredients will help push the fibers, leading to smooth and easy juicing. 3. Cleaning After juicing any ingredient or recipe, you may notice some residue left in the strainer and screw. This is normal because there is no pressure from other ingredients to push the final pieces out of the juicer. We recommend cleaning your juicer right away and soaking the parts in water, dish soap, and baking soda to remove the stains. Rub some of the paste that forms on the stains and let them sit for a few hours or overnight. For the screw, you can apply a little bit of vegetable oil to a paper towel and rub it onto the stained parts. We hope our tips worked and that you have a smoother juicing experience with ginger and turmeric. Thanks for watching!

segments.text: Tips for Juicing Ginger & Turmeric
Ginger and turmeric are commonly juiced in small amounts as part of a recipe
and typically pose no issues.
But, if you want to juice ginger and turmeric in larger amounts or on their own,
you may run into issues with clogging.
1. Ginger
Ginger and turmeric are fibrous ingredients with ginger being more fibrous than turmeric.
A buildup of their long stringy fibers can cause clogging.
The key is to chop ginger in the opposite direction of its fibers to break them up.
We recommend cutting ginger into small chunks, about 1-2 inches.
Juicing chopped ginger is quicker, more efficient,
and puts less strain on your juicer’s motor.
While juicing ginger, you may notice that juice produces quickly
and little pulp exits the spout.
This is normal.
Ginger is high in water content and light in weight,
so there's not much pressure pushing ginger pulp through the juicer.
2. Turmeric
Turmeric is smaller and less fibrous than ginger,
so it doesn’t need to be chopped.
But, it needs to be juiced with lemon
so your juicer doesn’t become heavily stained from the curcumin in turmeric.
You should have no issues juicing whole turmeric.
But in case you do, you can chop it using the same method as ginger.
As you saw, we juiced both ginger and turmeric with their peels intact.
You don’t need to peel them because their peels are thin and don’t cause clogging.
If you'd rather peel your ginger, using a spoon is a great option
as it can reach into the crevices and remove the skin without wasting the inner flesh.
Turmeric is a bit easier to peel, so it can be peeled normally with a peeler.
Just be mindful of turmeric staining your hands.
If you’re juicing a lot of ginger and turmeric with other ingredients,
layer them at the bottom of the hopper and place hard ingredients on top.
The hard ingredients will help push the fibers,
leading to smooth and easy juicing.
3. Cleaning
After juicing any ingredient or recipe,
you may notice some residue left in the strainer and screw.
This is normal because there is no pressure from other ingredients to push the final pieces out of the juicer.
We recommend cleaning your juicer right away
and soaking the parts in water, dish soap, and baking soda to remove the stains.
Rub some of the paste that forms on the stains
and let them sit for a few hours or overnight.
For the screw, you can apply a little bit of vegetable oil to a paper towel
and rub it onto the stained parts.
We hope our tips worked and that you have a smoother juicing experience with ginger and turmeric.
Thanks for watching!

videos.title: Healthy and Refreshing Pineapple and Ginger Drink - Pineapple Ginger Juice

transcript.fullText: hi everyone welcome back to my channel today we will be making this absolutely refreshing pineapple in ginger drink it is super healthy absolutely nutritious and easy to make let's begin for my ingredients i've got three pineapples they are a bit on the small side but that's okay i've also got some lemon and some turmeric which is optional i've also got some ginger you can use lime if you want but i find it a bit too sour so i'm going to stick with lemons instead and if you can't serve fresh turmeric you can use the powdered version instead so i'm going to start by preparing the pineapple and i'm just going to chop off the head and the bottom and then go ahead and peel the skin i should mention at this point that i will be using the pineapple skin as well as the chunks filters juice this is optional it's up to you if you want to use it but i should tell you that pineapple skin is very very nutritious and i'm just going to run through some of the benefits pineapple skin is very high in vitamin c and this is great for building the overall immunity of the body it helps to fight bacteria also strengthens your teeth and gums and also keeps your bones healthy and strong it also helps digestion and helps to relieve constipation and ibs it helps to heal arthritis and joint pains helps to battle eye diseases like glaucoma and also helps to prevent blood clots back to the pineapples after peeling i'll go ahead and cut off the pineapple coat that is the tough pit in the middle i won't be discarding them though because i will be using them in the recipe later after that i'll go ahead and chop the pineapples into small chunks so once i'm done with the pineapples i will transfer the skin and the core into a bowl and set this aside for later and i will continue with the rest of the pineapples off-camera the next thing to do is to peel the ginger as well as the turmeric the turmeric is not shown here but you will be seeing that later i love to use ginger for this recipe because it adds the spiciness to it you know this aromatic flavor that is so good it pairs so well with pineapple and it also has its own health benefits including the antibacterial powers it also helps to calm nausea it helps to lower blood sugar curbs the growth of cancer and also helps to ease arthritis and it's also got loads you know loads more benefit after chopping the ginger into small pieces i will be transferring this into my mortar in pound this is because i'm trying to release some of the juices we will be boiling this so we need it to be as potent as possible so i will be doing this another thing you can do is to blend but i just prefer to do this anyway and after doing this i will be setting it aside for use later the next thing to do is to juice the lemons like i said earlier you can use lime if you prefer to use that but now i will be using just just lemons so i will be chopping them into halves and then juice i used about six six for this recipe but it's up to you if you want to use more some people can tolerate more but i do suffer from acid reflux so i try to use as little as possible but this is okay for me once i'm done juicing the lemons i will be setting it aside for use later but the next thing to do is to wash the pineapple skin you need to do this because you need your drink to be as healthy as possible pineapple skin do contain a lot of dirt and debris and you really don't want that in your juice so i will be transferring that into this bowl and i'm also going to top with enough water now this next step is optional but i do like to add a bit of apple cider vinegar to my to my pan of skin before washing and this is because i just feel like it gets it a lot cleaner you can use plain vinegar as well if you want and using a brand new toothbrush i will be cleaning the pineapple skin thoroughly i'm done washing the pineapple skin i will be rinsing this i will be doing this twice just for good measure and that's it with the pineapple skin so it's time for the main event and we will be boiling the pineapple skin so i've got a pan here on medium heat and to that i will be transferring the pineapple skin as well as the coat i'm also going to add some tumeric as well and also some of the powdered ginger like i said earlier if you haven't got the fresh turmeric you can use the powder version i did add about a teaspoon of the powdered version as well at some point but that's fine so next i'm going to top up with enough water to cover after covering with water i'll stir and then cover and leave this to boil on medium heat for 15 minutes after 15 minutes i will turn the heat down to low and simmer for about 45 minutes to an hour this is what it looks like after it's done boiling and now i will take it off the heat and sieve if you're not pressed for time you can leave to sit for another hour and this way the juice is a lot more potent i should mention at this point that if you use the powdered um turmeric your juice may settle and this is because it just doesn't combine well enough and this is why i love to use the fresh one instead so after straining i will be setting it aside now go back to the pineapple chunks and i will be transferring those into my blender if you haven't got a blender you can use a hand blender instead or just anything to just juice it or you can use a juicer actually yeah so after transferring that into here i will be adding some of the ginger i'll also be covering with a bit of the juice you know from the pineapple skin and after adding the pineapple skin juice i will be covering and leave this to blend until smooth so the next thing to do is to strain the pineapple juice and for that i will be using a cheese cloth you can use a wire mesh instead but this works better for me it took a while though i think it took me about five minutes to to finish up i was trading it but no pain again right anyway so i will be doing this off camera this is what it looks like after straining as you can see it's got loads of foam on the top this is why you should always use a juicer instead but that's fine i will be straining this with a spoon and it will be just a square once the juice is strained i'm going to add the juice from the pineapple skin and then i'm going to add the lemon juice as well combine thoroughly and this is it this is our juice all done at this point you may choose to add sugar or any sort of sweetener that you like but it's up to you i prefer to use honey but not all the time because i use it more for medicinal purposes so this is how i love to enjoy it i just add some ice into a glass and add my juicing to it and that is it so if you do try this recipe please let me know how you get on and please don't forget to subscribe to my channel and also like this video please please please please and i hope to see you in my next video thanks bye

videos.title: How To Make Ginger Juice? Health benefits of ginger juice | Tamil Health Tv

transcript.fullText: How To Make Ginger Juice? Health benefits of ginger juice | Tamil Health Tv Ginger is a common ingredient we add in cooking and it has many medicinal properties. It will be hard to take ginger as pieces instead we can prepare ginger juice and drink. By drinking ginger juice we get numerous benefits and let us see how to prepare ginger juice in this video. Wash ginger, peel off the skin, cut it into pieces and grind it in a mixie adding required amount of water. Filter the ginger juice and squeeze half lemon in it. Then add little honey in it and mix well. Ginger juice is ready. By drinking this juice you can keep the sugar level under control so diabetic patients can take this. This juice protects the nervous system and keeps the brain healthy. The spiciness in the juice prevents and destroys cancer causing cells. It  is a remedy for stomach problems. It cures heartburn and reduces the unwanted cholesterol in our body. It keeps our heart healthy and cures knee pain.

videos.title: Make Any Probiotic Soda From Ginger

transcript.fullText: What if you could make this, this, and this with only this? These simple probiotic, gut- friendly sodas all start with a gingerbug. Let me show you how to make one. A gingerbug is a natural ferment made with ginger, sugar. Don't worry, most of this is going to be food for the bacteria, and water. Use some of this gingerbug each time you want to make a delicious fizzy soda like the ones I have here. ingredients. Ginger. Use organic. We don't want chemical residues that can harm the microbes and hinder fermentation. Ginger contains wild yeast and lactic acid bacteria along with those that are going to be in the environment on your utensils, your hands, whatever that will be the source of our probiotic culture. So, think good gut bacteria and good gut health. We need to feed these bacteria with our next ingredient, which is sugar. This can get complicated. There are a lot of sugars out there. So, this is not an exhaustive list, but here's a list of sugars you can use. These are all technically from sugar cane, typically from less refined to more refined. I use organic cane sugar because it's a good middle ground. It provides some minerals for our microbes, but not too many, which could actually slow down fermentation, especially in the beginning. Plain old white table sugar will also work fine. It doesn't necessarily have all the minerals, but it's a very nice clean source of sucrose that the bacteria actually will really love and you'll get some minerals from your water hopefully. So, don't feel like you can't use plain white table sugar. Don't use these because they don't contain the fermentable sugars needed to feed your microbes. So, no fermentation is going to happen. Raw honey is also not a great idea because it has antimicrobial properties and we're trying to not be anti to our microbes. So, we don't want to inhibit or kill the natural yeast and bacteria that we're trying to grow. I know you're going to ask me about maple syrup and coconut sugar. So, without taking a deep dive into their chemical compositions, these could be used sparingly, but they aren't great options. Same with molasses, which is actually commonly used alongside cane sugar. maybe a little pinch to add some minerals, but using too much can really overwhelm your gingerbug with just too many minerals and other compounds all at once. Lastly, water. Non-cllorinated water is crucial because chlorine can kill our natural bacteria. I use filtered water. You could use spring water or tap water. Just let it evaporate for 24 hours to decllorinate it. Reverse osmosis and distilled water aren't the best options because of the removal of minerals. Make a gingerbug step by step. No need to peel your organic ginger. Just wash it, chop it, grate it, or use a food processor like me. I then store my leftover ginger in the fridge so I can use it to feed my gingerbug later. Grab a clean glass jar. Add 2 cups of water with 2 tbsp of sugar and 2 tbsp of ginger. Now, you could make this smaller if you want. 1 cup of water with 1 tbsp of sugar and 1 tbsp of ginger. Just use that same ratio more or less. You don't even have to measure if you don't want to. I don't ever measure. Stir well. Then you're just going to cover loosely with a cloth, a coffee filter. I use a paper towel. I then screw the lid on and rip off my paper towel so that I can see what my gingerbug is doing. Unlike other fermentss, you do want this to breathe and get a little bit of oxygen. Then feed daily with the same amount of sugar and ginger. No need to add extra water for about 5 days. Stir daily to airate. You will start to see this bubbling at some point, which is a good sign of fermentation. The time this takes will vary depending on your ginger, the sugar you use, the temperature in your house, yada yada yada. And by the way, 70 to 75° F is a great temperature for your gingerbug. Now it's time to use this gingerbug to make some cool drinks. This is one I've had going for a while, so it's super bubbly and active. I usually pour a little bit of the gingerbug into a switch top bottle. This is a 16 oz. And then I add juice. I personally use pure organic juice, but you don't have to be that picky. Just watch out for additives that will stop your ferment. Use room temperature juice if possible. If it's cold from the fridge, it could drastically slow your ferment. Although, I've done it before and it will eventually work. I'm going to throw on this little sprouting lid to use as my strainer, but use whatever system works best for you. You could use a fine mesh strainer and rig up your own system. Funnel is always good to have as well. Now, if you like to measure more than I do, here's a good rule of thumb. Strain out and use 1 tbsp of your gingerbug liquid per one cup of juice. If you were paying attention, however, you noticed that I put in probably a little bit more, which brings me to an important point. By putting in more gingerbug, I'm probably going to get a faster ferment. So, you don't have to be super exact. Just give your juice some microbes. And don't worry about it too much. And here I am technically using a little less than 16 ounces of juice because you want to leave a good 1 to 2 in for expansion because this will get very bubbly when you open it. And now this just needs to sit at room temperature for anywhere from 12 hours to 3ish days depending on all the factors at hand. the strength of your gingerbug, the sugar content of your juice, the temperature in your house. Keep an eye on it and burp it just like this every 24 hours at least, maybe even more to make sure the pressure doesn't build up and you don't have a massive explosion because CO2 and some other gases are going to be building up inside of this jar. And use any kind of jar if you don't care about bubbles. Use a regular mason jar, put a top on it loosely and you won't get all the CO2 buildup. Or you can put the lid on tightly and you will, but just make sure again to loosen the lid, open it for a second, let some of the CO2 out so that you don't break this. You will get more bubbles than you can get from a can of soda. I'm telling you. Now, you probably got some questions like, "How do I maintain this thing? How do I use it to make other fun drinks?" So, I have more videos for you in order to not overwhelm you completely in this one. They're linked below. Be sure to tell me what you make with your gingerbug. And remember to eat real food.

transcript.fullText: hi everyone welcome back again to dubsy's delight today i'm back with you with another delicious and healthy fresh homemade juice so if you want to see how i made this deliciousness please keep watching i'll begin by peeling off the skin of my pineapple please know that i wash this very well with some brush my chicken brush of course some white vinegar and some more water as i'm going to boil the skin so it needs to be very clean as possible i'll then transfer the skin to my pot [Music] [Music] the dark spots or dots on the pineapple can cause your throat and mouth to itch so i make sure that when i'm making my juices i cut enough off i'll then chop my pineapple into half and then quarters just to make sure that i cut off the hard core in the pineapple as i'm not going to blend it with it i'll then transfer the hard core to the pot and add enough water about half cup of brown sugar and set it on fire to boil of course if you do not like sugar you can always use honey but when i'm making this juice i don't like the strong taste the honey adds to the juice so i stick to brown sugar it's always down to preference okay while that boils away i'll continue prepping the ingredients so i'll just chop the pineapple into tiny chunks as i'll be blending it later i'll then introduce my ginger which i've washed very well and chop it as well as i always mention when i'm making my juices i do not peel my ginger but you can always peel it as it does add a little bit of bitterness to your juice so if you don't like it please go ahead and peel it videos.title: Drink Carrot And Ginger Juice Each Morning, THIS Will Happen To Your Body!

transcript.fullText: carrots and ginger are two healthy food choices that can provide our bodies with many types of nutrients and promote our health many ways both have incredible immunity boosting benefits can promote digestion and reduce the risk of certain diseases to make your own carrot and ginger juice at home all you will need is four to five large carrots a one inch piece of fresh ginger and half of a peeled orange for taste and even more immunity boosting effects make sure you wash the carrots and ginger and then simply place all of the ingredients into a juicer or blender and enjoy now let's discuss some of the amazing health benefits of drinking carrot and ginger juice first consuming carrot and ginger juice can help to improve digestion and relieve unwanted digestive ailments carrots are packed with fiber especially pectin a soluble fiber the great levels of fiber and nutrition found in carrots can also help to promote the health and growth of good bacteria in the gut leading to improved digestion and nutrient uptake as well carrots can help to reduce constipation and promote regularity ginger is also great for digestion and often consumed for this reason ginger can help to treat and reduce symptoms associated with indigestion it can also promote stomach emptying reduce bloating and stomach pain increase nutrient uptake reduce nausea and promote regularity carrot and ginger juice can also help to naturally boost immunity and reduce symptoms associated with the common cold and flu carrots contain potent plant compounds such as carotenoids which have been linked to improved immune function and the reduction of certain illnesses and degenerative ailments the tremendous amounts of antioxidants and carrots such as beta-carotene lutein and lycopene are also helpful for boosting our immune system ginger is also great for our immune system and can provide powerful medicinal properties this is because ginger contains a powerful bioactive compound known as gingerol that has tremendous anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects which can reduce free radicals that can damage the immune system as well ginger contains antibacterial properties which can help with the elimination of bad bacteria in the body and reduce the risk of certain infections lastly if you are looking to lower your cholesterol levels and improve your overall heart health then drinking carrot and ginger juice can certainly help studies have shown that regular consumption of carrots can help to lower total cholesterol levels and bad ldl cholesterol ginger can also help to specifically lower cholesterol levels and improve heart health studies have shown that when ginger was consumed regularly there was a significant drop in bad ldl cholesterol total cholesterol levels and triglycerides as well it should be noted that carrot and ginger juice can help with weight loss and proper weight management due to its good levels of fiber its ability to promote satiety and balance blood sugar levels and is quite low in calories thank you for watching the video please like comment and share if you would like to hear about more healthy foods and food and beauty tips please subscribe to the channel have a great and healthy day [Music]

videos.title: How to Make Fresh Apple Lemon Ginger Juice

transcript.fullText: hi I'm Christina Jackson natural food chef and holistic health coach we're in the kitchen today juicing it up and we're going to make apple ginger lemon juice today we're going to grab our apples lemons and Ginger today I selected pink lady apples they're one of my personal favorites because they're super sweet and juicy you can obviously substitute them for red delicious or Granny Smith if you want something a little more tart but um we're going to start with the apples I'm going to grab those Pink Ladies here we're going to grab a lemon as well one lemon usually to two apples I like that ratio it adds just that bit of tartness I'm looking for and fresh ginger about an inch of fresh ginger if you want it spicier go ahead and add it in there's no worries to that um remember to start with Organic ingredients organic is fresher when you're juicing you want the freshest ingredients in your juice because you're enjoying all those immediate nutrients that you're getting from from your fruits and vegetables so let's get started and cut these in quarters when you are juicing the apples with the skin on it is going to be a little cloudier but remember it's going to be more nutritious [Music] I love the way it smells all right now that we have our apples in let's add our Ginger and put that in there it's not going to yield a ton of juice but it's going to add just that right bit of spiciness to your juice the great thing about Ginger is it helps aid in digestion all right let's add our lemon take the lemon and you want to roll it on the counter first to get the juice away from the Rind let's cut it in half to make it fit in the juicer a lot easier now you can throw it in just like this because it's going to give you more nutrients that are located in the Rind and in the white membranes here um that you don't typically uh in ingest so let's throw it in there all right this is going to be delicious and adding the lemons are going to make it a detoxifying drink lemons are high in vitamin C and they're great detoxifier so you have your sweet detoxifying drink here natural sugars natural sweetness it's delicious and nutritious for you you're going to want to make sure you drink it though 20 minutes after you make it that's a good number that way you're absorbing the most nutrients and vitamins and minerals and enzymes there that are available to you and then if you you must take it or take it with you or save it go ahead and put it in a airtight container I like to use just a good old mason jar here that usually does the trick uh put it in there throw it in your refrigerator you want to keep it refrigerated and it should last you about 24 hours if not go ahead and enjoy it as a nice pickme up detoxifying drink and that's how you make a apple lemon ginger juice enjoy thanks for watching be sure to catch all of our other videos here and don't forget to subscribe because that way we'll keep you updated on all the other healthy delicious videos we'll be putting out if you have any requests for me or any questions email me at request at mahalo.com see you next time

videos.title: Drink Beetroot Ginger Juice Every Day – Health Benefits You Didn’t Know!

transcript.fullText: They say the best medicine is made by nature and sometimes it is just a glass away. Beetroot and ginger. Two powerful ingredients. One healing drink. This is what happens when you start drinking beetroot ginger juice. First, it boosts blood circulation. Beetroot is rich in natural nitrates which help relax and widen your blood vessels. The results, lower blood pressure and better oxygen flow to your brain, heart and muscles. That means more energy, more stamina, and even better brain function. Ginger brings its own magic. Powerful compounds called ginger rolls. They fight inflammation, soothe joint pain, and calm your digestive system. Together, beetroot and ginger supports natural detox, cleansing your liver and helping your body flush out toxins effortlessly. And let's not forget your skin. The antioxidants in beetroot help slow aging and give you glowing skin. Ginger promotes collagen production, leaving your skin firm, smooth, and radiant. Athletes love beetroot juice for a reason. It increases endurance and helps your muscle perform longer with less fatigue. All you need is this. One small beetroot, a thumb of fresh ginger, a little water, juice it fresh, and drink it daily on an empty stomach. Your body will thank you. Nature gives us everything we need to heal. You just have to say yes, one sip at a time. Don't forget to like this video, share with a friend, and subscribe to Simply Natural for more videos about living a healthy lifestyle and living in harmony with nature. Thank you for watching. [Music]
